response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of software engineering experience and strong fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems, multi-threading via DLang fibers), but the role requires a hands-on C++ developer with industrial automation depth. The only direct C/C++ experience is a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and an academic thesis project. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or Azure cloud work. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not align with the core technical competencies.
strengths: Senior-level tenure (~15+ years) with experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput service work (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — only ~6 months at National Instruments a decade ago
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No industrial automation, MQTT, or industrial protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)

key_factors.factor: Strong senior engineering experience and systems fundamentals
